name: Recipe: Mongolian Beef
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a cleaver to slice beef across grain and at an angle into thin strips.
2. Combine marinade ingredients in a medium bowl. Add beef strips; mix well. Let stand at least 1 hour.
3. Heat oil in a wok over high heat to 350°F (175°C). Gently loosen roll of rice noodles with your fingers and break into 2 portions.
4. Carefully lower half the noodles into hot oil with a slotted metal spoon or in a large strainer and press under oil for 2 seconds. Immediately remove puffed noodles from wok with slotted spoon or strainer; set aside to cool.
5. Repeat with remaining half of rice noodle roll. Break cooled noodles into 1-inch lengths and arrange on a platter.
6. Remove oil from wok except 5 tablespoons (70ml). Heat remaining oil over high heat for 30 seconds.
7. Stir-fry marinated beef until very lightly browned. Remove with slotted spoon; set aside.
8. Add hoisin sauce, hot bean sauce, cornstarch, and water to the remaining oil in wok. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
9. Add green onions and cooked beef. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
10. Spoon mixture over noodles. Makes 4-6 servings.
